Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials/Methods states NatBURP1 primers were based on NIATv7_g20816/XP_019248232.1, and later says NatBURP1 is available as A0A1J6ITY3 (UniProt) and NIATv7_g20816.

Source_Species.reason: NatBURP1 is explicitly described as the lyciumin precursor gene from N. attenuata.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly uses Nicotiana attenuata for NatBURP1.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Materials and Methods lists N. attenuata seeds as UT31X, and NatBURP1 cloning used the N. attenuata UT31X genome.

Applied_Species.reason: NatBURP1 was transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied host is explicitly Nicotiana benthamiana.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: NatBURP1 is directly linked to burpitide/lyciumin biosynthesis in Results and Fig. 1.

Enzyme_Name.reason: NatBURP1 is described as a burpitide precursor whose BURP domain acts as peptide cyclase for lyciumin formation; this label is faithful though partly interpretive.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: NatBURP1 contains a BURP domain, and the paper discusses BURP domains as autocatalytic peptide cyclases.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state lyciumins P and Q were identified in N. attenuata roots consistent with the NatBURP1 chemotype.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state NatBURP1 was syringe-infiltrated/transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used for NatBURP1-related validation in Results: LC-MS root profiling, heterologous expression, chimeric/site-directed mutant analyses, and MS/MS.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results support that NatBURP1 mediates lyciumin cyclization as a burpitide precursor/cyclase, though exact bond wording is inferred from lyciumin class rather than directly re-elucidated here.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Fig. 3A and Fig. 4B explicitly use lyciumin core peptide [QPWGVYSW] with the lyciumin cyclase.

Primary_Product.reason: NatBURP1 expression produced lyciumins P and Q in N. benthamiana and these were detected in N. attenuata roots.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: NatBURP1/BURP domain performs cyclization, but the paper explicitly says full lyciumin biosynthesis likely also requires peptidases and glutaminyl cyclotransferases; calling it the terminal step is too strong.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: NatBURP1 is specifically associated with the lyciumin branch/chemotype of burpitide biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The caveat 'rate-limiting status not established' matches the paper, and NatBURP1 is shown to determine lyciumin chemotype.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: NatBURP1 is directly tested and directly linked to lyciumin formation in heterologous expression.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results and Fig. 1C identify lyciumins P and Q as NatBURP1-associated products.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Lyciumins are presented as cyclic peptides/burpitides, a class of RiPPs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Lyciumins P and Q were found in N. attenuata roots and produced upon NatBURP1 expression in N. benthamiana leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper does not establish a specific ecological function for lyciumins here; the cautious wording is faithful.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: NatBURP1 expression promotes production of lyciumins P and Q in the heterologous assay.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each summary point is supported: endogenous association with root lyciumins, heterologous reconstitution, broader/promiscuous activity toward nanamin core peptides, and five-residue swaps altering specificity.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves is explicitly described.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results state mutant cyclases had reduced catalytic efficiency; no broader tradeoffs were reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion highlights engineering value of these macrocyclases and burpitide systems for novel peptides/agronomic traits.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper specifies the pEAQ-HT vector/system, but does not identify a promoter by name in the text provided.

Omics_Data.reason: The statement carefully says no NatBURP1-specific omics accession was generated here; transcriptome assembly context applies elsewhere in the study.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly state full lyciumin biosynthesis likely involves peptidases and glutaminyl cyclotransferases beyond the BURP cyclase component.

Source_Species.reason: ΨNatBURP2 is identified as a locus in Nicotiana attenuata.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly refers to N. attenuata / Nicotiana attenuata.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Materials and Methods lists N. attenuata accession/seed line UT31X.

Applied_Species.reason: The repaired construct 5x_Nna was transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The host species is explicitly Nicotiana benthamiana.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: 5x_Nna is directly shown to produce nanamin, a burpitide cyclic RiPP.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The repaired pseudogene encodes the nanamin precursor/cyclase activity within a BURP-domain burpitide precursor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is a BURP-domain protein and the BURP domain is discussed as an autocatalytic peptide cyclase.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results explicitly say ΨNatBURP2 is pseudogenized in N. attenuata and nanamin is not detected in N. attenuata extracts.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: 5x_Nna was transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are directly described for 5x_Nna: pseudogene repair, heterologous expression, LC-MS/MS, purification/NMR, alanine scan/core peptide mutagenesis, and chimeric swaps.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly determines a C–C bond between tyrosine phenolic metacarbon and glycine α-carbon in nanamin produced by 5x_Nna.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results and Fig. 3 show native [QPWGLMY] and many engineered variants tested with the nanamin cyclase; glycine and tyrosine are required.

Primary_Product.reason: 5x_Nna expression produced nanamin A; nanamin B is described as an oxidized form.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: As with lyciumin, pyroglutamate formation and processing likely involve host enzymes, so calling the BURP reaction the terminal step is not fully supported.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The gene is specifically tied to the nanamin branch/chemotype.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports chemotype-determining role, and the field explicitly says rate-limiting status not established.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: 5x_Nna directly generates nanamin in heterologous expression.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Nanamin A is the principal product directly reported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Nanamin is a cyclic peptide burpitide, within plant RiPPs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: 5x_Nna produced nanamin in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, and related wild-type chemotype occurs in roots of N. clevelandii/N. pauciflora.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper does not establish a specific ecological function and highlights engineering/drug-discovery potential.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Repair and expression of 5x_Nna enabled nanamin production and specified its cyclization chemistry.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All five summary statements are directly supported by Results.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana is described, and spontaneous oxidation to nanamin B during purification is explicitly noted.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The native locus is pseudogenized in N. attenuata with no nanamin accumulation, and mutant cyclases are reported to have reduced catalytic efficiency.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly emphasizes broad substrate tolerance and value for engineering peptides with challenging C–C macrocycles.

Applied_Promoters.reason: pEAQ-HT vector is explicit, but no promoter is named in the provided text.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state repeat number was reduced from 11 to 5 for synthesis and discuss likely host involvement in pyroglutamate formation/processing.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: De novo transcriptome assembly section states sequences for NclBURP2 are available with NCBI as PV067132.

Source_Species.reason: NclBURP2 is identified from Nicotiana clevelandii.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: NclBURP2 is explicitly designated the nanamin precursor gene and linked to nanamin biosynthesis.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Calling it a BURP-domain burpitide/nanamin precursor cyclase matches the paper's functional characterization.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: NclBURP2 is a BURP-domain precursor in the same family as other burpitide cyclases.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transcriptome assembly, gene identification, transient expression, and LC-MS metabolite profiling are all explicitly described.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results state NclBURP2 exhibited the same activity as 5x_Nna, i.e., nanamin production.

Primary_Substrate.reason: As a precursor/cyclase it necessarily carries an encoded nanamin core peptide, but the exact substrate wording 'core peptide(s)' is not explicitly detailed for NclBURP2.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: As above, the BURP cyclase is central but processing/pyroglutamate maturation involve other enzymes, so 'terminal step' is too strong.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It is directly the nanamin branch precursor gene.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The study does not establish a biological function for nanamin.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves is the assay used.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering value of nanamin cyclases generally is discussed, but NclBURP2 specifically as a 'natural intact source' is a reasonable inference rather than an explicit result.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transcriptome assembly plus metabolite correlation is exactly what was done; there was no direct functional assay.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is inferred from homology/chemotype association, not directly tested for NclBURP1 in Results.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Even for validated BURP genes, 'terminal step' is too strong; for NclBURP1 this is only inferred.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It is identified as a lyciumin precursor gene, so the lyciumin branch assignment is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Lyciumins were detected in the species, but not directly connected to NclBURP1 by expression assay.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: No specific function was established in this study.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential utility is speculative and not directly discussed for NclBURP1 specifically.

Omics_Data.reason: Transcriptome assembly sources and PV067131 deposition are stated in Methods.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This caveated statement is faithful: NclBURP1 is identified explicitly, but direct functional validation in this paper focused on NclBURP2, not NclBURP1.
